# JOS-S54 — Task Definition Doc-Standard

## Purpose

Lock the shape of a Task Definition. Every JOS Task Definition conforms.

A Task Definition is the **I/O-contract substrate** — it specifies what
a unit of work takes as input, what it produces as output, and how to
verify the work was correctly done. Task Definitions are referenced by
Project Templates (`suggested_task_definitions`), instantiated into
live `pm_tasks` rows, and consumed by agents + humans alike.

## Boundary test (before authoring)

You're authoring a Task Definition ONLY IF:

- The work has a definable I/O contract (input schema, output schema,
  or both — at least one).
- The work has at least one acceptance criterion that can be
  mechanically verified.
- The work is **reusable** — the same Task Definition will be
  instantiated more than once with different inputs.

If the work is a one-time engineering ask → that's a `pm_task`, not a
Task Definition.

If the work is a multi-step procedure → that's an SOP, not a Task
Definition. (An SOP may reference Task Definitions for its individual
steps, but the SOP itself is the procedure, the TD is the unit.)

If the work has no verifiable acceptance criteria → defer authoring
until you can name at least one.

## Author voice

- **Declarative I/O.** "Takes X, produces Y, verifiable by Z."
- **Atomic.** A Task Definition is one unit. If you need three
  consecutive steps, that's an SOP composing three Task Definitions.
- **Tool/engine-aware.** If the work requires a specific tool or
  engine, name it in `uses_tools` / `uses_engines` so the platform
  can pre-check availability.

## Required frontmatter

| Field | Required | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `id: "JOS-TD-###"` | YES | 3-digit sequence |
| `uuid: "<v4>"` | YES | |
| `title: "<Short TD Title>"` | YES | |
| `type: task_definition` | YES | |
| `owner: <name>` | YES | |
| `solve_for: > <paragraph>` | YES | what gap this TD closes |
| `stage_label:` | YES | one of: pilot / trial / adopt / deprecated |
| `flexibility:` | YES | typically `expected` |
| `domain:` | YES | |
| `provenance_tier:` | YES | |
| Universal cascade fields | YES | |
| `references:` | OPTIONAL | typed cross-refs |

## Required body sections

The Task Definition body MUST include the following fields. Optional
fields default sensibly when omitted.

| Field | Required | Type | Meaning |
|---|---|---|---|
| `purpose` | YES | prose | One paragraph: what the task accomplishes. |
| `scope_boundary` | YES | prose | What this task does NOT cover (helps avoid scope creep). |
| `acceptance_criteria` | YES | list[dict] | ≥1 entry. Each entry: see below. |
| `input_schema_id` | OPTIONAL (≥1 of input/output required) | UUID | Library Schema item that validates the task input. |
| `output_schema_id` | OPTIONAL (≥1 of input/output required) | UUID | Library Schema item that validates the task output. |
| `default_assignee_kind` | OPTIONAL | `agent` / `human` | Hints which side typically does this work. |
| `estimated_effort_hours` | OPTIONAL | float | Operator-facing time estimate. |
| `uses_tools` | OPTIONAL | list[str] | Tool registry IDs the task needs. |
| `uses_engines` | OPTIONAL | list[str] | Engine registry IDs the task needs. |
| `connected_objects` | OPTIONAL | list[dict] | Typed cross-references. |

### `acceptance_criteria[]` entry shape (per LIB-FRAMEWORK-002)

Each entry MUST include:

| Field | Required | Type | Meaning |
|---|---|---|---|
| `criterion_id` | YES | str (URL-safe slug) | Stable identifier; auto-slug from criterion text if not provided. |
| `criterion` | YES | prose | One-sentence acceptance bar. May include `{{slot_label}}` interpolation. |
| `verifiable_by` | YES | prose | How the criterion is mechanically verified (tool, query, file existence, etc.). |
| `consumes_slots` | OPTIONAL | list[str] | Slots this criterion depends on. Used by the validator to detect orphaned slots. |

## Composition (per JOS-C51)

Task Definitions MAY declare `references:` for related substrate, but
they do NOT have a typed `implements:` field — they are leaves, not
implementations. The composition story is **outbound**: Project
Templates reference Task Definitions via `suggested_task_definitions`,
and SOPs may reference them in their `connected_objects`.

## Anti-patterns

- **Task Definition with 0 acceptance criteria.** Without an acceptance
  bar, the work cannot be verified done. Either define one or convert
  to a free-form `pm_task`.
- **Task Definition with multi-step `criterion` text.** "Do A, then B,
  then C" inside a single criterion is a smell — that's an SOP.
  Decompose into 3 acceptance criteria or author an SOP.
- **`uses_tools` / `uses_engines` populated with unregistered IDs.**
  Validator emits a warning for unknown IDs; before bless, all entries
  should resolve to the tool / engine registry.
- **Both `input_schema_id` and `output_schema_id` left null.** A Task
  Definition with no I/O contract is just prose; the platform can't
  validate anything. Author at least one.
- **Slot label in prose without a `consumes_slots` declaration.**
  Validator (BIND-002 / BIND-003 from JOS-S56) catches this — every
  `{{label}}` in `criterion` or `verifiable_by` must also appear in
  the entry's `consumes_slots[]`.

## Worked examples

### Example — minimal Task Definition
```python
purpose: "Draft a customer service response from a ticket."
scope_boundary: "Drafts only — does not send. Send is a separate task."
acceptance_criteria: [
    {
        "criterion_id": "uses-correct-tone",
        "criterion": "Response uses the {{response_tone}} tone.",
        "verifiable_by": "LLM-as-judge sentiment classifier",
        "consumes_slots": ["response_tone"],
    },
    {
        "criterion_id": "within-length-limit",
        "criterion": "Response is ≤ {{max_response_length_words}} words.",
        "verifiable_by": "word count assertion",
        "consumes_slots": ["max_response_length_words"],
    },
]
input_schema_id: <UUID of input schema>
output_schema_id: <UUID of output schema>
default_assignee_kind: agent
uses_tools: []
uses_engines: ["llm_roster"]
```

## Validation

Mechanical checks (implemented in
`src/work_definition/definitions/type_validators/task_definition_validator.py`):

| Rule | Enforces |
|---|---|
| TD-CRIT-BASE | Every `acceptance_criteria[]` has `criterion` + `verifiable_by`. |
| TD-CRIT-001 | Every `acceptance_criteria[]` has `criterion_id` (URL-safe slug, unique). |
| TD-CRIT-002 | `uses_tools[]` and `uses_engines[]` are validated against registry (warn if unknown). |
| BIND-001..004 | Slot-binding rules from JOS-S56 apply to `criterion` + `verifiable_by` fields. |
